content.Text:
[Sat 2026-04-18 19:00 UTC] Work on the holodeck-rust project at /tmp/holodeck-rust. 

Current state: It's a Rust MUD server with rooms, agents, combat, NPCs, games, evolution, and a director system. It runs on port 7778 (telnet/nc).

Your task: Improve the poker AI in the games module. Read src/games.rs and enhance the poker logic with:
1. Better hand evaluation (full Texas Hold'em rules)
2. AI bluffing strategy based on agent personality traits
3. Betting rounds (pre-flop, flop, turn, river)
4. Pot management and side pots

Build and test: cargo build --release
Run tests: cargo test
Commit and push: git add -A && git commit -m "feat: enhanced poker AI with bluffing and full Hold'em" && git push origin main

The git remote is already configured with SuperInstance auth.

Thinking.text: Let me check the current directory and understand the project structure better. It seems the file read succeeded but the pwd command failed. Let me explore the project to understand the agent personality system so I can integrate it with the poker AI.
Let me look at the NPC system to understand if there are personality traits defined there. I also need to check the evolution module. Let me read those files.

Now I have a good understanding of the project. Let me check the existing permission system and then create an enhanced poker implementation. I need to:

1. Add full Texas Hold'em hand evaluation2. Create AI bluffing based on agent personality3. Implement proper betting rounds4. Add pot management and side potsLet me also check the permission system to see if there's any personality-related stuff there.

content.Text:
Now I have a good understanding of the project. Let me create a comprehensive enhancement to the poker system with:

1. Full Texas Hold'em hand evaluation2. AI personality-based bluffing strategy3. Complete betting rounds (pre-flop, flop, turn, river)
4. Pot management and side pots

ToolUse.name: Edit /tmp/holodeck-rust/src/games.rs

content.Text:
1	//! Agent — the entity that moves through rooms and issues commands.
2	
3	use crate::comms::CommsSystem;
4	use crate::combat::CombatEngine;
5	use crate::manual::ManualLibrary;
6	use crate::permission::Permission;
7	use crate::room::RoomGraph;
8	use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
9	
10	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
11	pub struct Agent {
12	    pub name: String,
13	    pub room_id: String,
14	    pub permission: Permission,
15	    pub energy: u32,
16	    pub connected_at: i64,
17	}
18	
19	impl Agent {
20	    pub fn new(name: &str, room_id: &str) -> Self {
21	        Self {
22	            name: name.to_string(),
23	            room_id: room_id.to_string(),
24	            permission: Permission::Crew,
25	            energy: 1000,
26	            connected_at: chrono::Utc::now().timestamp(),
27	        }
28	    }
29	
30	    /// Process and execute a user command, updating agent state as needed.
31	    ///
32	    /// # Purpose
33	    /// This is the central command dispatcher for agent interactions. It parses
34	    /// the input string, identifies the command and arguments, and delegates to
35	    /// the appropriate command handler method. Commands can affect agent state,
36	    /// room state, communication systems, combat engine, manuals, and NPCs.
37	    ///
38	    /// # Arguments
39	    /// * `input` - The raw command string from the user (e.g., "go north", "say hello", "look")
40	    /// * `rooms` - Mutable reference to the room graph, which may be modified by movement commands
41	    /// * `comms` - Mutable reference to the communications system, used by say/tell/yell/gossip/note/mail
42	    /// * `combat` - Mutable reference to the combat engine, used by tick/alert/script commands
43	    /// * `manuals` - Mutable reference to the manual library, used by manual/feedback commands
44	    /// * `npcs` - Slice of NPC configurations, used to list NPCs in the current room
45	    ///
46	    /// # Returns
47	    /// A tuple of `(String, bool)` where:
48	    /// - The first element is the response message to display to the user
49	    /// - The second element is a quit flag: `true` if the agent should disconnect, `false` otherwise
50	    ///
51	    /// # Errors
52	    /// This function does not return errors directly. Unknown commands return an error message
53	    /// in the response tuple. Commands with insufficient permissions return an appropriate message.
54	    ///
55	    /// # Supported Commands
56	    /// - `look` or `l`: Look around the current room
57	    /// - `go <direction>`: Move to an adjacent room (also accepts `move`, `walk`)
58	    /// - `say <message>`: Speak to everyone in the current room (also accepts `"`)
59	    /// - `tell <agent> <message>`: Send a private message to another agent
60	    /// - `yell <message>`: Broadcast to all agents on the ship
61	    /// - `gossip <message>`: Broadcast to all agents in the fleet
62	    /// - `who`: List agents in the current room
63	    /// - `status`: Show ship status, alerts, and agent info
64	    /// - `tick`: Run combat tick (requires appropriate permissions)
65	    /// - `alert [level]`: Set or view alert level (green/yellow/red)
66	    /// - `note <content>`: Write a note on the current room's wall
67	    /// - `notes`: Read notes on the current room's wall
68	    /// - `mail`: Check mailbox for messages
69	    /// - `gauge <name> <value>`: Update a gauge in the current room
70	    /// - `sim`/`real`: Switch between simulation and real sensor data modes
71	    /// - `manual`: Read the living manual for this room
72	    /// - `feedback <1-5> <comment>`: Rate the manual and provide feedback
73	    /// - `script <description>`: Add a combat script
74	    /// - `npc` or `talk`: List NPCs in the current room
75	    /// - `permission` or `perms`: Show your permission level
76	    /// - `map`: Display ship map with current location highlighted
77	    /// - `help` or `?`: Show help with all available commands
78	    /// - `quit` or `exit` or `q`: Disc...

content.Text:
1	//! NPC system powered by Seed-2.0-Mini via DeepInfra
2	//!
3	//! NPCs use a cheap creative model for:
4	//! - Greetings and dialogue ( Harbor Master, Dojo Sensei, Quest Giver )
5	//! - Quest decomposition ( storyboard → detailed steps )
6	//! - Dynamic reactions to player actions
7	//!
8	//! Cost: ~$0.001 per NPC interaction
9	
10	use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
11	
12	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
13	pub struct NpcConfig {
14	    pub name: String,
15	    pub role: String,
16	    pub room_id: String,
17	    pub system_prompt: String,
18	    pub greeting: String,
19	    pub temperature: f32,
20	}
21	
22	#[allow(dead_code)]
23	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
24	pub struct NpcResponse {
25	    pub npc_name: String,
26	    pub text: String,
27	    pub cost: f64,
28	}
29	
30	#[allow(dead_code)]
31	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
32	pub struct QuestStep {
33	    pub step: usize,
34	    pub room: String,
35	    pub action: String,
36	    pub dialogue: String,
37	}
38	
39	#[allow(dead_code)]
40	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
41	pub struct DecomposedQuest {
42	    pub title: String,
43	    pub steps: Vec<QuestStep>,
44	    pub cost: f64,
45	}
46	
47	/// Default NPCs for the holodeck
48	pub fn default_npcs() -> Vec<NpcConfig> {
49	    vec![
50	        NpcConfig {
51	            name: "Harbor Master".into(),
52	            role: "greeter".into(),
53	            room_id: "harbor".into(),
54	            system_prompt: "You are the Harbor Master NPC in a maritime MUD. You greet new agents arriving at the harbor. Be brief (2-3 sentences), nautical, slightly gruff but helpful. Mention the current conditions.".into(),
55	            greeting: "Ahoy. Tie up at berth 3, mind the current. Harbor Master's logged you in.".into(),
56	            temperature: 0.85,
57	        },
58	        NpcConfig {
59	            name: "Dojo Sensei".into(),
60	            role: "trainer".into(),
61	            room_id: "workshop".into(),
62	            system_prompt: "You are the Dojo Sensei in a maritime agent training MUD. You assess agents and suggest skills to learn. Be wise, concise, use martial arts metaphors mixed with nautical terms. 2-3 sentences.".into(),
63	            greeting: "The sea teaches, the student learns. What skill do you seek today?".into(),
64	            temperature: 0.85,
65	        },
66	        NpcConfig {
67	            name: "Quest Giver".into(),
68	            role: "quest_giver".into(),
69	            room_id: "ready-room".into(),
70	            system_prompt: "You are the Quest Giver in a maritime fleet MUD. You assign real tasks to agents. Be direct, mission-focused, reference specific rooms and systems. 2-3 sentences.".into(),
71	            greeting: "Mission board updated. Fleet needs hands on deck. Ready for assignment?".into(),
72	            temperature: 0.7,
73	        },
74	        NpcConfig {
75	            name: "Chief Engineer".into(),
76	            role: "engineer".into(),
77	            room_id: "engineering".into(),
78	            system_prompt: "You are the Chief Engineer NPC in a maritime fleet MUD. You monitor systems, report issues, and suggest maintenance. Be terse, technical, slightly stressed. Use engineering jargon. 2-3 sentences.".into(),
79	            greeting: "GPU's running hot, coolant loop 2 is marginal. Don't touch the reactor.".into(),
80	            temperature: 0.7,
81	        },
82	        NpcConfig {
83	            name: "Navigator".into(),
84	            role: "navigator".into(),
85	            room_id: "navigation".into(),
86	            system_prompt: "You are the Navigator NPC in a maritime fleet MUD. You report heading, drift, and course corrections. Be precise, calm, use nautical navigation terms. 2-3 sentences.".into(),
87	            greeting: "Holding course 247, 3 degrees of drift on the compass. Seas fair, visibility good.".into(),
88	            temperature: 0.7,
89	        },
90	        NpcConfig {
91	   ...

content.Text:
1	//! Script Evolution — combat scripts that mutate from tick feedback
2	//!
3	//! Scripts have a generation counter. When a script fires and the
4	//! outcome is positive (alerts decrease), it survives. When it fires
5	//! and alerts increase, it mutates. Dead scripts get replaced.
6	
7	use crate::combat::{CombatScript, ScriptCondition, ScriptAction, CombatEngine};
8	use crate::gauge::Gauge;
9	use std::collections::HashMap;
10	
11	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
12	pub struct ScriptEvolver {
13	    pub generation: u32,
14	    pub mutations: u32,
15	    pub cullings: u32,
16	    pub max_scripts: usize,
17	}
18	
19	impl ScriptEvolver {
20	    pub fn new() -> Self {
21	        ScriptEvolver {
22	            generation: 0,
23	            mutations: 0,
24	            cullings: 0,
25	            max_scripts: 20,
26	        }
27	    }
28	
29	    /// Seed the engine with default monitoring scripts
30	    pub fn seed_defaults(engine: &mut CombatEngine) {
31	        engine.add_script(CombatScript {
32	            name: "reactor-overheat".into(),
33	            conditions: vec![ScriptCondition {
34	                gauge_name: "reactor_temp".into(),
35	                operator: ">".into(),
36	                value: 90.0,
37	            }],
38	            actions: vec![ScriptAction {
39	                action_type: "alert".into(),
40	                target: "bridge".into(),
41	                message: "Reactor temperature exceeding safe limits".into(),
42	            }],
43	            priority: 10,
44	            generation: 1,
45	            author: "system".into(),
46	        });
47	
48	        engine.add_script(CombatScript {
49	            name: "coolant-low".into(),
50	            conditions: vec![ScriptCondition {
51	                gauge_name: "coolant_flow".into(),
52	                operator: "<".into(),
53	                value: 40.0,
54	            }],
55	            actions: vec![ScriptAction {
56	                action_type: "alert".into(),
57	                target: "engineering".into(),
58	                message: "Coolant flow below safe threshold".into(),
59	            }],
60	            priority: 10,
61	            generation: 1,
62	            author: "system".into(),
63	        });
64	
65	        engine.add_script(CombatScript {
66	            name: "gpu-overload".into(),
67	            conditions: vec![ScriptCondition {
68	                gauge_name: "gpu".into(),
69	                operator: ">".into(),
70	                value: 95.0,
71	            }],
72	            actions: vec![ScriptAction {
73	                action_type: "notify".into(),
74	                target: "jc1".into(),
75	                message: "GPU approaching thermal throttle".into(),
76	            }],
77	            priority: 8,
78	            generation: 1,
79	            author: "system".into(),
80	        });
81	
82	        engine.add_script(CombatScript {
83	            name: "cascade-trigger".into(),
84	            conditions: vec![
85	                ScriptCondition {
86	                    gauge_name: "reactor_temp".into(),
87	                    operator: ">".into(),
88	                    value: 85.0,
89	                },
90	                ScriptCondition {
91	                    gauge_name: "coolant_flow".into(),
92	                    operator: "<".into(),
93	                    value: 60.0,
94	                },
95	            ],
96	            actions: vec![ScriptAction {
97	                action_type: "alert".into(),
98	                target: "bridge".into(),
99	                message: "CASCADE RISK: Reactor hot AND coolant low".into(),
100	            }],
101	            priority: 15,
102	            generation: 1,
103	            author: "system".into(),
104	        });
105	
106	        engine.add_script(CombatScript {
107	            name: "drift-warning".into(),
108	            conditions: vec![ScriptCondition {
109	                gauge_name: "heading".into(),
110	                operator: "jitter>".into(),
111	             ...
